symptoms.symptom_name: Greasy yellow scale on erythematous patches of the central face

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Greasy yellow scale on erythema in sebaceous skin is the hallmark morphology.
Lupus malar rash is a fixed erythema without greasy scale and typically spares the nasolabial folds.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical distribution in nasolabial folds, eyebrows and anterior scalp margin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral symmetrical sebaceous-gland distribution is characteristic of seborrhoeic dermatitis.
Cellulitis is characteristically unilateral and spreading, not symmetrical.
Scalp seborrhoea (dandruff) is part of the same disease spectrum and commonly co-occurs with facial involvement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic relapsing course with intermittent milder episodes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A chronic-relapsing pattern strongly favours seborrhoeic dermatitis over an acute infection or a first-presentation autoimmune rash.
Cellulitis is an acute one-off infection, not a years-long relapsing pattern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Trigger by physiological stress and cold, dry weather

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stress and cold dry weather are classic flare triggers for seborrhoeic dermatitis.
Barrier disruption from harsh alkaline soap aggravates seborrhoeic dermatitis; also identifies a modifiable behaviour to correct.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, no unilateral hot/painful rapidly spreading area (pertinent negative for cellulitis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of systemic upset and of unilateral spreading warmth/induration makes cellulitis unlikely — though telehealth cannot palpate, so it is not fully excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: No joint pain, mouth ulcers or photosensitivity (pertinent negative for lupus)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of systemic autoimmune features reduces the likelihood of lupus behind the facial erythema.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of deep dermal induration or fluctuance on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician palpation confirming no induration/fluctuance helps exclude cellulitis — a sealed, clinician-only finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Negative fungal culture / KOH (if performed in atypical cases)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative dermatophyte culture/KOH helps exclude tinea in atypical presentations; not acutely indicated for this classic picture and never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Greasy yellow scale + symmetrical sebaceous distribution + scalp dandruff + chronic-relapsing course together make seborrhoeic dermatitis the confident clinical diagnosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin scraping for fungal microscopy / culture (KOH)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated for a classic seborrhoeic dermatitis flare; reserve for atypical or treatment-resistant cases to exclude tinea. Over-ordering here is unnecessary.

No systemic autoimmune features are present; testing on the basis of the patient's fear alone would be low-yield. Recommend only if joint pain, mouth ulcers, photosensitivity or a sparing malar rash emerge.
